title: Colymbada murbeckii
taxonomy: Colymbada murbeckii is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Colymbada murbeckii belongs to the subfamily Cichorioideae. Within the subfamily Cichorioideae, Colymbada murbeckii is placed in the tribe Cichorieae, which includes many species of dandelions and related plants. The genus Colymbada is a small group of plants that are closely related to the genus Crepis, and Colymbada murbeckii is one of the species within this genus. Further classification of Colymbada murbeckii may involve more specific taxonomic levels, such as species group or species complex.

intro: Colymbada murbeckii, also known as the Sardinian Centaury or Centaurea murbeckii, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Colymbada murbeckii include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Colymbada murbeckii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Colymbada murbeckii is often prepared in the form of tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create medicinal syrups and salves.
